mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand, which translates to higher corporate earnings and thus positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors anticipate improved financial performance.
failure_condition: If RSAFS month-over-month growth is positive but SPX 20d returns are negative in more than 50% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as economic weakness becomes apparent.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.5pp over 3 months, but SPX 60d returns are positive in more than 40% of such triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is rising significantly while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains low, it suggests the Federal Reserve is behind the curve, leading to expectations of future aggressive tightening and negative equity market sentiment over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: low interest rate, rising inflation environment
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% and FEDFUNDS is below 1%, but SPX 20d returns are consistently positive (Rank IC > 0.05) in such periods.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) indicate robust economic activity and future demand, which is particularly bullish for equities during periods of economic recovery, signaling sustained growth and leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If HOUST exceeds 1500.0k units during a recovery, but SPX 5d returns show no significant positive correlation (p > 0.1) with HOUST levels.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects healthy manufacturing and supply chain activity, which underpins corporate profitability and supports positive long-term equity market performance over a 60-day horizon as earnings expectations improve.
failure_condition: If INDPRO shows consistent positive month-over-month growth, but SPX 60d returns are negative in more than 50% of such periods over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals a strengthening labor market, boosting consumer confidence and spending, which drives corporate revenues and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by more than 200,000 persons month-over-month
failure_condition: If PAYEMS increases by over 200k, but SPX 20d returns are negative in more than 45% of triggered instances over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth (GDPC1) is strong but the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is kept low, it indicates an accommodative monetary policy supporting robust economic expansion, which is highly favorable for equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
regime_hint: early-to-mid cycle expansion with accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: If GDPC1 growth exceeds 3% and FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%, but SPX 60d returns show a negative or statistically insignificant correlation (p > 0.1) in such periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in inflation (CPIAUCSL) during a period of already high equity valuations can trigger fears of aggressive monetary tightening, leading to a sharp, short-term negative reaction in the S&P 500 over a 5-day horizon as investors reprice risk.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL month-over-month change exceeds 0.5% in a high valuation environment, but SPX 5d returns are positive in more than 50% of such instances.
